channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Shaheen, Tillis and Bennet tour Ukraine amid Trump peace push
item.description: Sens. Jeanne Shaheen (D-N.H.), Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) and Michael Bennet (D-Colo.) all recently took a trip to Ukraine according to a page on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee's website. The senators finished the visit to Ukraine’s capital city, Kyiv, on Monday, according to the page.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels tighten their grip on second major city in eastern Congo
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels tightened their grip on Bukavu on Monday, a day after seizing the second major city in eastern Congo whose residents appeared resigned to their fate under the new rulers.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL CINAMULA and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz says he's 'in touch with Delta' after crash landing at Toronto airport
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) said Monday he was “in touch with Delta” following the crash landing of a flight at Toronto Pearson International Airport. The flight originated at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport (MSP) in Walz's state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump stuns Europe with opening Ukraine-Russia gambit
item.description: European leaders are scrambling to respond to President Trump’s first moves to end Russia’s war in Ukraine, shocked to find themselves on the outside of high-stakes talks about the continent’s security and grappling with a potential retreat of U.S. forces from Europe.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Graham: 'Very little appetite' for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said Monday he thinks there's "very little appetite" among Americans for a U.S. takeover of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine presses Europe to nominate representative for Russia talks
item.description: Ukraine is urging Europe to quickly select a representative for potential talks with the U.S. and Russia on ending Kyiv's war with Russia. “It should be a quickly made decision,” Igor Zhovkva, the deputy head of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s office, told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Many weary Ukrainians long for an end to the war but now fear it will come on unfavorable terms
item.description: After nearly three years of living under constant threat of Russian airstrikes while their troops fight a grinding campaign against Russia’s invasion, many Ukrainians long for an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: Mexico says it’s awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taiwan cheers removal of ‘we do not support Taiwan independence’ from State Department website
item.description: Taiwan expressed approval after the U.S. State Department removed a statement on its website that said it does not support the island's independence. The phrase “we do not support Taiwan independence” was removed from a department fact sheet last week. The document, which highlights U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates on Monday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war on the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Conservative Party leader in Canada pledges to respond forcefully to Trump tariffs
item.description: Pierre Poilievre, the leader of Canada’s Conservative Party, pledged on Saturday to respond forcefully to tariffs imposed by President Trump that harm the Canadian people or their economy.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy not yet signing US economic agreement 'short-sighted,' White House official says
item.description: A White House national security official said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "is being short-sighted" in not signing a rare earth minerals deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military experts weigh in on Trump's ‘all hell’ threat to Hamas and what it could look like
item.description: Former Israeli military officials discuss how President Donald Trump's threats against Hamas could play out in Gaza, and what strategies the IDF could adopt if they go back into the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World's first openly gay imam shot dead in South Africa: authorities
item.description: Muhsin Hendricks, a man known as the world's first openly gay imam, was killed in South Africa over the weekend after being ambushed near the city of Gqeberha.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At least 18 people dead after stampede at New Delhi railway station
item.description: “The crowd went out of control and no one could control it,” said Nikhil Kumar, a shopkeeper who witnessed the crowd surge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu will reportedly hold a security meeting at Trump's deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu is expected to hold a security meeting at 12 p.m. eastern, President Trump's deadline for Hamas to release the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Crunch time for hostages amid ceasefire uncertainty
item.description: Fox News Correspondent Greg Palkot spoke with the daughter and sister of two released hostages. Both noted the positive role played by President Trump in helping to free their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages in exchange for more than 300 prisoners as part of ceasefire deal with Israel
item.description: Hamas freed three more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
